attractions.name: Spectra – Light & Water Show
attractions.description: A free nightly performance of dancing fountains, colorful projections, and music set against the bay skyline
attractions.hours: Daily at 8:00 PM and 9:00 PM, with an additional 10:00 PM show on Fridays and Saturdays
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: Singapore Cable Car
attractions.description: Scenic ride with panoramic city and ocean views floating towards Sentosa Island
attractions.hours: First ride: 8:45 AM | Last boarding: 9:30 PM
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf
Singapore and Malaysia/All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: Skyline Luge Sentosa
attractions.description: Engage in an adrenaline rush where you'll whiz down purpose-built tracks on a gravity-fueled cart—fun for both adults and kids
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: Harry Potter: Visions of Magic
attractions.description: An immersive, multi-sensory journey filled with spellbinding sets, interactive technology, and atmospheric soundscapes that transport you straight into the wizarding world
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: Universal Studios Singapore
attractions.description: Theme park with popular rides like Battlestar Galactica: HUMAN vs. CYLON, Transformers: The Ride 3D, and Jurassic Park Rapids Adventure
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: Fort Canning Park

source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: Singapore Botanic Gardens

attractions.name: Marina Bay Street Circuit

attractions.name: ArtScience Museum
attractions.description: Singapore's iconic lotus-shaped landmark by the bay. Inside, wander through strikingly designed galleries where art, science, technology, and culture intersect. The highlight for many is FUTURE WORLD: Where Art Meets Science, a permanent exhibition created with teamLab
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: Wings of Time
attractions.description: Breathtaking outdoor night show combining pyrotechnics, fountains, lasers, and storytelling against ocean backdrop
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: National Orchid Garden
attractions.description: Over 1,000 species and 2,000 hybrids of orchids in bloom
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

attractions.name: Singapore Flyer
attractions.description: Giant observation wheel offering panoramic views of the city
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

hotels.name: Hotel Boss
hotels.location: Hotel Boss stands in a vibrant crossroads of culture and convenience. It sits snugly between Kampong Glam (Arab Street) and Little India, offering immediate access to rich heritage sites, artsy cafes, and local boutiques

local_dishes.name: Vegetarian Laksa
local_dishes.description: Rich coconut curry noodle soup with tofu puffs, bean sprouts, and mock seafood
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Vegetarian Hainanese Chicken Rice
local_dishes.description: Singapore's national dish in a vegetarian twist with mock meat or tofu
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Vegetarian bee hoon
local_dishes.description: Singaporean noodle dish that contains vegetarian spring rolls, fried tofu skin, and mock meats made from gluten. It also comprises of white rice noodles that are either thick or thin in texture
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Char Kway Teow (Veg version)
local_dishes.description: Flat rice noodles wok-fried with soy sauce, veggies, and egg (optional)
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Roti Prata with Curry
local_dishes.description: Crisp Indian flatbread served with dhal (lentil curry) or vegetable curry
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Satay (Veg skewers)
local_dishes.description: Plant-based skewers grilled over charcoal, served with peanut sauce
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Carrot Cake (Chai Tow Kway)
local_dishes.description: Stir-fried radish cake with egg, preserved radish, and soy sauce (black or white version)
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf
Singapore and Malaysia/All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Popiah (Fresh Spring Roll)
local_dishes.description: Paper-thin wrap filled with stewed turnip, veggies, and peanuts
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Masala Dosa
local_dishes.description: Crisp rice-lentil crepe filled with spiced potatoes, served with chutneys
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Vegetarian Briyani
local_dishes.description: Aromatic rice dish with spices, vegetables, and raita
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Gado-Gado
local_dishes.description: Indonesian-style salad with peanut sauce, tempeh, tofu, and veggies
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Sayur Lodeh
local_dishes.description: Light coconut vegetable curry, a Malay classic
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Kaya Toast with Kopi/Teh
local_dishes.description: Toast with coconut jam & butter (vegan versions available)
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Ice Kachang
local_dishes.description: Shaved ice mountain with syrup, beans, corn, and jelly (ask for vegetarian toppings)
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Chendol
local_dishes.description: Sweet, icy and coconutty dessert that is made with coconut milk, palm sugar and shaved ice. It also contain green, jelly noodles made with rice flour
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Vegan Chicken Rice

local_dishes.name: Thunder Tea Rice

local_dishes.name: Prata
local_dishes.description: Roti prata is fried flatbread, and good ones are both crispy and fluffy. Ask for 'prata kosong' which means plain prata and let them know you do not eat egg
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Singapore- All Inclusive Guide - Vishal(2).pdf

local_dishes.name: Stir fried Kai lan
local_dishes.description: It refers to Chinese broccoli, that is stir-fried and then tossed with a little oil to give it a smooth glossy sheen

local_dishes.description: Chilled drink that is made with condensed milk and rose cordial syrup, giving it a pink colour

local_dishes.name: Popiah
local_dishes.description: Fresh spring rolls stuffed with veggies wrapped in thin rice sheets. It is best served with a sweet sauce (often a bean sauce) or hot chilli sauce

local_dishes.name: Red bean buns
local_dishes.description: Red bean paste is a popular sweet and moreish filling for many bread-type of desserts. Pancakes, buns and waffles can be stuffed with this paste, which has a thick, reddish-black colour
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/Singapore- All Inclusive Guide - Vishal(2).pdf

local_dishes.name: Potato curry puff
local_dishes.description: The humble curry puff is a popular snack which is vegetarian-friendly

local_dishes.name: Hainanese Chicken Rice
local_dishes.description: Poached chicken served with fragrant rice cooked in chicken stock, with chili sauce and ginger paste

local_dishes.name: Chili Crab
local_dishes.description: Fresh crab stir-fried in a tangy, sweet, and spicy tomato-chili sauce
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Satay
local_dishes.description: Grilled skewers of marinated meat served with peanut sauce, ketupat (rice cakes), and cucumber-onion sides
source_files: Singapore and Malaysia/All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

local_dishes.name: Hokkien Mee
local_dishes.description: Stir-fried egg noodles and rice noodles with prawns, squid, and rich seafood stock

local_dishes.name: Roti Prata
local_dishes.description: Crispy yet fluffy Indian flatbread, often served with curry

local_dishes.name: Kaya Toast
local_dishes.description: Toasted bread with coconut jam (kaya) and butter, paired with soft-boiled eggs and soy sauce

connectivity_tips.tip: Best Tourist SIMs: Singtel hi!Tourist, StarHub Travel Prepaid, M1 Tourist SIM. Buy at Changi Arrival Hall (Changi Recommends) or 7-Eleven. Passport required. All offer strong 4G coverage; M1 has best data pricing
connectivity_tips._source_file: Singapore and Malaysia/Copy of All Inclusive Guide_Singapore.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: There are 3 main providers- Singtel, StarHub and M1. Singtel's tourist card is hi!Tourist SIM card, StarHub's is StarHub Travel Prepaid and M1 is M1 Tourist SIM Card. They are all equal in terms of coverage. M1 offers the best data packs in terms of price and data allowance.
connectivity_tips._source_file: Singapore and Malaysia/Singapore- All Inclusive Guide - Vishal(2).pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: The best place to buy a prepaid sim card is in the airport on arrival from the Changi Recommends Kiosks at Arrival Hall, Level 1. If you choose not to get it here, you can buy it from a Seven-Eleven convenience store
connectivity_tips._source_file: Singapore and Malaysia/Singapore- All Inclusive Guide - Vishal(2).pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: There are 3 main providers- Singtel, StarHub and M1. Singtel's tourist card is hi!Tourist SIM card, StarHub's is StarHub Travel Prepaid and M1 is M1 Tourist SIM Card. They are all equal in terms of coverage. M1 offers the best data packs in terms of price and data allowance.
connectivity_tips._source_file: Singapore and Malaysia/Singapore- All Inclusive Guide - Vishal(1).pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: The best place to buy a prepaid sim card is in the airport on arrival. If you choose not to get it here, you can buy it from a Seven-Eleven convenience store
connectivity_tips._source_file: Singapore and Malaysia/Singapore- All Inclusive Guide - Vishal(1).pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Best Tourist SIMs: Singtel hi!Tourist, StarHub Travel Prepaid, M1 Tourist SIM
Buy SIM cards at Changi Arrival Hall (Changi Recommends) or 7-Eleven
Passport required for SIM card purchase
All offer strong 4G coverage; M1 has best data pricing

There are 3 main providers- Singtel, StarHub and M1. M1 offers the best data packs in terms of price and data allowance. The best place to buy a prepaid sim card is in the airport on arrival from the Changi Recommends Kiosks at Arrival Hall, Level 1. If you choose not to get it here, you can buy it from a Seven-Eleven convenience store
connectivity_tips._source_file: Singapore and Malaysia/Singapore- All Inclusive Guide - Vishal.pdf
